p: A comprehensive digital ecosystem for modern educational institutions

UniTrack is an educational management platform that revolutionizes how schools and universities operate. Built with modern web technologies and powered by artificial intelligence, it provides a unified digital environment for students, teachers, parents, and administrators.
🏫 Multi-Institutional Support- Seamlessly manage K-12 schools and universities
🤖 AI-Powered Analytics- Intelligent insights and personalized recommendations
📱 Progressive Web App- Works offline with native app-like experience
⚡ Real-Time Communication- Instant messaging and notifications
📊 Advanced Reporting- Automated PDF reports with AI-generated summaries
🔐 Enterprise Security- JWT authentication with role-based access control
Role-Based Access Control- Students, Teachers, Parents, Admins, Super Admins
Multi-Institutional Membership- Users can belong to multiple institutions
Automated Onboarding- Streamlined registration and approval workflows
Flexible Academic Structure- Support for grades, faculties, departments, majors
Multiple Grading Systems- Bulgarian (2-6), American (GPA), European (ECTS)
Course Management- Core subjects and elective courses with enrollment tracking
Academic Calendar- Configurable semesters and academic years
Grade Management- Topic-based grading with detailed descriptions
Attendance Tracking- Real-time check-in/check-out with excuse management
AI-Powered Insights- Predictive analytics and personalized recommendations
Comparative Analysis- Institution benchmarking and market trends
Real-Time Messaging- Direct and group chats with file attachments
Event Management- Academic, social, and sports event coordination
Smart Notifications- Automated alerts for grades, attendance, and events
Parent Integration- Real-time updates on student progress
Automated PDF Generation- Professional reports with AI summaries
Executive Dashboards- KPI tracking with visual analytics
Scheduled Reports- Automated periodic report generation
🎨 UI Library: Naive UI + Tailwind CSS
🔄 State Management: Pinia + Pinia Colada for data fetching
📱 PWA: Offline-first with service worker caching
🗄️ Database: PostgreSQL with Entity Framework Core
🤖 AI/ML: Qdrant vector database + Ollama local models
🔐 Security: JWT Bearer + ASP.NET Core Identity
Streamlined Administration- Reduce paperwork with digital workflows
Data-Driven Decisions- AI insights for strategic planning
Enhanced Communication- Connect all stakeholders seamlessly
Compliance Ready- Support multiple educational standards
Efficient Grade Management- Quick entry with intelligent analytics
Student Progress Tracking- Identify at-risk students early
Automated Reporting- Generate professional reports instantly
Real-time Collaboration- Connect with colleagues and parents
Real-time Updates- Instant notifications on student progress
Direct Communication- Chat with teachers and school staff
Event Participation- Stay informed about school activities
Academic Insights- Understand learning patterns and trends
Predictive Modeling- Forecast academic performance trends
Risk Detection- Early identification of students needing support
Semantic Search- Natural language queries across all data
Performance Benchmarking- Compare against regional/national standards
Trend Analysis- Identify patterns in academic and operational data
This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

UniTrack was developed as a Bachelor's thesis project at the Technical University of Sofia, Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Informatics by Spas Dimitrov Milenkov under the supervision of Assoc. Prof. Dr. Zlatko Zahariev.
Thesis Title:"Web Application for Management and Analysis of Educational Processes in Educational Institutions"

l: ⭐ Star this repository if you like the concept of UniTrack
p: Made with ❤️ for the future of education

l: Educational Management System - Database Schema Breakdown

p: The system consists of 5 main functional areas that work together to manage educational institutions:
Core Entities: 16 tables Purpose: Manages all user types, authentication, and relationships
Identity management with ASP.NET Core Identity
Core Entities: 18 tables Purpose: Defines the academic hierarchy and course structure
Core Entities: 6 tables Purpose: Tracks student performance, attendance, and grading
Flexible marking system with topic-based grades
Core Entities: 7 tables Purpose: Facilitates communication and event management
Core Entities: 4 tables Purpose: Generates insights and performance reports
This educational management system provides a comprehensive platform covering:
User Management: 16 tables managing authentication and user roles
Academic Structure: 18 tables defining institutional hierarchy
Performance Tracking: 6 tables for grades and attendance
Communication: 7 tables for messaging and events
Analytics: 4 tables for reporting and insights
Total: 51 database tables working together to create a complete educational ecosystem.

This document outlines the production architecture for the UniTrack application, a modern web application with integrated AI capabilities built using microservices architecture and containerized deployment.
The entry point for all system interactions. Regular users access the application through the web interface, while administrators have additional access to monitoring and observability tools.
Users connect via HTTPS on port 8080
Administrators access monitoring tools directly on dedicated ports
Components: Nginx Load Balancer, UniTrack Frontend
This layer handles all user-facing interactions and traffic distribution.
Provides SSL termination for secure connections
Routes traffic between frontend and backend services
Handles load balancing across multiple backend instances
Serves as the single entry point for external traffic
Nuxt.js application providing the user interface
Communicates with backend services via /api routes
The core business logic layer implemented as scalable ASP.NET Core services.
High Availability: Two instances provide redundancy and load distribution
API Gateway: Centralizes business logic and data access
Authentication Integration: Connects with Firebase for user management
Configured with 2 replicas for production workloads
Can be scaled horizontally based on demand
Abstraction layer that groups related services and manages service dependencies.
Centralizes access to all data storage systems
Provides unified interface for backend applications
Manages connections to relational, vector, and AI services
Designed for future external service integrations
Components: PostgreSQL Database, Qdrant Vector Database, Ollama LLM Service
The persistence and AI processing layer.
Primary relational database for application data
Stores user information, application state, and transactional data
Configured with health checks and automatic restart policies
Specialized vector database for AI/ML operations
Supports both REST (6333) and gRPC (6334) APIs
Handles vector similarity search and embeddings storage
Local large language model inference service
Provides AI capabilities without external dependencies
Runs on port 11434 with persistent model storage
Scrapes metrics from all application services
Connects to Prometheus for data queries
Components: Database Volume, Vector Storage Volume, Model Storage Volume
Persistent storage management for all stateful services.
Vector Storage Volume: Qdrant vector data and indices
Model Storage Volume: Ollama language models and configurations
Provides service discovery and DNS resolution
Isolates application traffic from external networks
Multiple backend instances with load balancing
Automatic restart policies for service recovery
SSL termination at the load balancer
Resource limits and reservations for optimal performance
Backend Scaling: Configured with 2 replicas, can be increased based on load
Resource Limits: AI services have memory constraints (Ollama: 8GB, Qdrant: 2GB)
Storage Optimization: Dedicated volumes for each data service
Network Efficiency: Overlay network for optimized inter-service communication
This architecture provides a robust, scalable, and maintainable foundation for the UniTrack application with integrated AI capabilities.
Domain-Driven Design- Each service handles a specific business domain
Separation of Concerns- Clear boundaries between different functionalities
Business Logic Encapsulation- Core business rules and processes
Service Dependencies- Services can depend on repositories and other services
Repository Pattern- Abstraction over data access logic
Entity Framework Core- ORM for database operations
Clean Separation- Models, repositories, and context are separated
Reusable Components- Common base classes and interfaces

Separation of Concerns- Clear boundaries between different functionalities

Service Dependencies- Services can depend on repositories and other services
Repository Pattern- Abstraction over data access logic
Entity Framework Core- ORM for database operations

🎯 Server API routes- Full-stack in one project
Configure the .NET Core API with all required integrations and services
The UniTrack backend is built with .NET 8.0 and provides:
🤖 AI Integration with Ollama and Qdrant vector database
📧 Email Services via Gmail OAuth2 or Mailtrap
📁 File Storage through Firebase Cloud Storage
Username and password (set in Docker environment)
Jwt.Audience: Same as issuer for simple setup
Create new project or select existing
No API key required for local deployment
Create a .env file for production deployment:
appsettings.json configured with all required settings
Gmail OAuth2 credentials obtained and configured
Configure the Nuxt.js Progressive Web Application with modern features and integrations
The UniTrack frontend is a modern Nuxt.js application featuring:
⚡ Nuxt 3 with Vue.js 3 Composition API and TypeScript
📱 Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline capabilities
🎨 Modern UI with Naive UI components and Tailwind CSS
📊 Data Visualization with Chart.js and OpenLayers mapping
📈 Performance Optimized with SSR, caching, and code splitting
Create the following files in public/:
Maskable Icons: For better PWA integration
Generate VAPID keys for push notifications
Environment variables set for all environments
PWA icons generated and placed in public/
Tailwind CSS configured with custom theme
🎊 Congratulations! Your UniTrack frontend is now fully configured and ready for deployment!

Health checks pass for all services
A step-by-step guide to deploy UniTrack's full-stack educational management platform
A domain(for production) or localhost for development
Your project should be organized as follows:
This setup guide is split into three detailed configuration sections:
You'll need to obtain the following before deployment:
JWT Secret Key(generate a random 256-bit key)
Mailtrap(Host, Port, Username, Password) - for development
Ollama Models- Downloaded automatically on first run
Qdrant- No API key needed for local deployment
Configure Docker Infrastructure- Set up the container orchestration
Configure Backend API- Set up the .NET Core API with all integrations
Configure Frontend- Set up the Nuxt.js Progressive Web App
Once all three sections are complete, you'll have a fully functional UniTrack deployment! 🎉
Continue to the next section: Docker Infrastructure Setup
